questions.q: What does SQL stand for?
Which SQL clause is used to filter records?
What does the JOIN clause do?
options: Combines rows from two or more tables

questions.q: Which type of JOIN returns all rows when there is a match in either table?
What is a primary key?
options: A unique identifier for each record in a table

questions.q: Which aggregate function returns the number of rows?
What does GROUP BY do?
options: Groups rows sharing a property so aggregate functions can be applied

questions.q: What is the difference between WHERE and HAVING?
options: WHERE filters rows before grouping; HAVING filters after grouping

questions.q: Which statement is used to insert new data into a table?
What does DISTINCT do in a SELECT query?
What is a foreign key?
options: A primary key in another table
A field that links to the primary key of another table

questions.q: Which command removes all rows from a table without logging?
What is a subquery?
Which SQL function returns the current date?
options: All of the above depending on DBMS

questions.q: What does the LIKE operator do?
Which wildcard represents zero or more characters in LIKE?
What is normalization in databases?
options: Organizing data to reduce redundancy and dependency

questions.q: What does ALTER TABLE do?
Which constraint ensures all values in a column are different?
What is an INDEX used for?
What does UNION do?
options: Combines results of two SELECT statements removing duplicates

questions.q: What is the difference between UNION and UNION ALL?
options: UNION removes duplicates; UNION ALL keeps them
UNION only works with two tables

questions.q: Which clause limits the number of rows returned?
What is a VIEW in SQL?
options: A virtual table based on a SELECT query

questions.q: What does CASCADE do in a foreign key constraint?
options: Automatically applies changes to related rows

questions.q: Which statement updates existing data in a table?
What is a transaction in SQL?
options: A unit of work that is either fully completed or fully rolled back

questions.q: What does COMMIT do?
options: Saves all changes made during the current transaction

questions.q: What does ROLLBACK do?
options: Undoes changes made during the current transaction

questions.q: What is a stored procedure?
options: A precompiled collection of SQL statements stored in the database

questions.q: Which type of JOIN returns only matching rows from both tables?
What is a NULL value in SQL?
How do you check for NULL values?
What does ORDER BY do?
Which keyword sorts in descending order?
What is a trigger in SQL?
options: Code that automatically executes in response to certain events on a table

questions.q: What does COALESCE() do?
options: Returns the first non-NULL value in a list

questions.q: What is the purpose of the BETWEEN operator?
options: Selects values within a given range

questions.q: What is a composite key?
options: A primary key made up of two or more columns

questions.q: Which function converts a value to uppercase?
options: Both A and B depending on DBMS

questions.q: What is denormalization?
options: Intentionally adding redundancy to improve read performance

questions.q: What does EXISTS do in a subquery?
options: Returns TRUE if the subquery returns one or more rows

questions.q: What is a self-join?
options: Joining a table with a view

questions.q: Which clause is used with aggregate functions to filter groups?
What is ACID in database transactions?
What does DROP TABLE do?
options: Removes the table and all its data permanently

questions.q: What is the difference between DELETE and TRUNCATE?
options: DELETE removes specific rows with WHERE; TRUNCATE removes all rows and is faster

questions.q: What is a cursor in SQL?
options: A pointer to navigate through result set rows one at a time

questions.q: Which function returns the length of a string?
What is a CTE (Common Table Expression)?
options: A temporary named result set defined within a query using WITH
